full_prompt: Design the primary wing spar of a SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular class aircraft. Regular class constraints: wingspan b <= 2.5 m, takeoff distance <= 61 m (200 ft), maximum take-off weight (MTOW = empty + payload) optimized to maximize payload score per the SAE Brasil scoring formula. For this design target: wingspan 2.4 m, rectangular planform with chord 0.32 m, NACA 4412 airfoil, empty weight (We) 3.2 kg, payload weight (Wp) 12 kg, giving MTOW 15.2 kg. Cruise speed 16 m/s at sea level ISA (density 1.225 kg/m^3). Takeoff loads drive spar sizing because the full 15.2 kg load is supported at ~ 1.2 g near liftoff with maximum dynamic pressure on the wing. The spar is a rectangular-section hollow box beam running the full span, fabricated from balsa caps + carbon pultruded-rod caps + 1 mm plywood web sandwich, simplified in FEA as a homogeneous transversely isotropic beam with effective E 35 GPa, tensile 400 MPa, compressive 350 MPa, density 650 kg/m^3 (composite average). Spar nominal cross-section 30 mm x 15 mm x 2 mm wall-equivalent. Wing-root joins the fuselage via two M4 steel bolts. Load cases: (LC1) static 1 g trim with elliptical lift distribution across 2.4 m span supporting 15.2 kg (total lift 149 N); (LC2) +2.5 g pull-up at dive speed (limit): total lift 373 N elliptical; (LC3) ultimate 1.5 x LC2 = 3.75 g equivalent, total 559 N - per SAE Brasil structural conservative factor; (LC4) landing hard-impact 3 g on MLG attached to fuselage while wings are in 1 g trim; (LC5) ground gust wingtip 80 N horizontal; (LC6) scoring-weight: SAE Brasil Regular payload scoring is (Wp)^2 / (We) - a design optimization metric; spar must survive the declared MTOW, not scored directly here. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC3 ultimate, max composite stress must remain below 200 MPa (400 / 2.0 FoS) in tension and 175 MPa (350 / 2.0) in compression; (R2) wingtip vertical deflection under LC1 must remain below 2% of half-span (24 mm); (R3) first wing bending natural frequency must exceed 6 Hz to clear servo bandwidth; (R4) spar dry mass must not exceed 0.45 kg to preserve We budget; (R5) root bolt bearing stress under LC3 ultimate must remain below 350 MPa. Thermal, fluid, radiation analyses not required; buckling check captured by compressive stress gate at this slenderness. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of spar, composite cap stack-up (balsa + pultruded rod schedule), FEA for LC1-LC5, modal check, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary wing spar of a SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular-class aircraft. Regular class: span <= 2.5 m, takeoff distance <= 61 m, MTOW optimized via the SAE Brasil scoring formula. Design target: 2.4 m span x 0.32 m chord NACA 4412, empty 3.2 kg + 12 kg payload = 15.2 kg MTOW, cruise 16 m/s at sea level.

Envelope: rectangular hollow box beam balsa caps + carbon pultruded rods + 1 mm plywood web sandwich; simplified FEA as homogeneous transversely-isotropic beam 30 x 15 mm with 2 mm wall-equivalent; wing root bolted to fuselage with 2 x M4 steel bolts.

Factor regime: SAE Brasil ultimate FoS 2.0 (200 MPa tensile / 175 MPa compressive); wingtip deflection at 1 g trim <= 2% of half-span (24 mm); first wing-bending mode > 6 Hz; spar dry mass <= 0.45 kg; root bolt bearing at ultimate <= 350 MPa.

Load cases.
- Static 1 g trim: 149 N elliptical lift over 2.4 m span.
- +2.5 g pull-up at dive speed (limit): 373 N elliptical lift.
- Ultimate 1.5 x limit = 3.75 g: 559 N elliptical lift.
- 3 g hard landing on MLG at the fuselage with wings in 1 g trim.
- Ground-gust wingtip 80 N horizontal.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: SAE Brasil AeroDesign Regular scoring formula (Wp)^2 / (We) is an outer-loop design optimizer that calls the structural FEA to validate each MTOW candidate.

prompt.title: SAE Brasil AeroDesign 2025 Regular Class Wing Spar
prompt.brief: Balsa + pultruded-CF composite box-beam wing spar, 2.4 m span, 15.2 kg MTOW, +2.5 g limit, ultimate 1.5x, SAE Brasil FoS.

material.name: Balsa + pultruded-CFRP caps + plywood web (composite effective)

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam with orthotropic composite properties; TET10 near root bolt region

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero reduced to elliptical lift distribution.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Short span + low slenderness; stress gate bounds.
